title: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil

seo.title: Classic Hummus Recipe: Easy & Authentic Middle Eastern Dip
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best classic hummus recipe at home! This authentic Middle Eastern dip with tahini and olive oil is creamy, easy, and perfect for any occasion. Get the full recipe now!
longTailKeywords: homemade hummus with chickpeas and olive oil
lowCompetitionKeywords: hummus with tahini and olive oil
seo.h1: Classic Hummus Recipe: Authentic Middle Eastern Tahini Dip
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Homemade Hummus
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic hummus drizzled with olive oil, garnished with paprika and parsley, served with pita bread.
seo.imageCaption: Whip up this incredibly creamy and flavorful classic hummus, perfect for dipping or spreading!

description: Experience the rich, creamy texture and vibrant flavors of authentic classic hummus. This beloved Middle Eastern dip combines chickpeas, tahini, lemon, and garlic for a truly satisfying appetizer or spread. Drizzled generously with olive oil, it is a simple yet elegant dish perfect for any gathering.

instructions.title: Prepare Chickpeas
instructions.text: If using canned chickpeas, drain them and rinse thoroughly under cold water. For extra creamy hummus, you can gently rub the chickpeas between your hands to remove some of the skins, though this is optional. Set aside.

instructions.title: Blend Tahini Paste
instructions.text: In a food processor, combine the tahini, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, and salt. Process for about 1-2 minutes until the mixture becomes pale and thick. This emulsifies the tahini and lemon, creating a smooth base.

instructions.title: Add Chickpeas and Cumin
instructions.text: Add the drained chickpeas and ground cumin (if using) to the tahini mixture in the food processor. Process for another 1-2 minutes until the chickpeas are mostly broken down and the mixture starts to look crumbly.

instructions.title: Achieve Creaminess with Ice Water
instructions.text: With the food processor running, slowly drizzle in the ice water, a tablespoon at a time. Continue to process until the hummus becomes incredibly smooth and creamy. You may need to stop and scrape down the sides of the bowl occasionally.

instructions.title: Adjust Seasoning
instructions.text: Taste the hummus and adjust the seasoning as needed. You might want to add more salt, lemon juice, or even a tiny bit more tahini to achieve your desired flavor profile. Process again briefly to incorporate.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Transfer the creamy hummus to a serving bowl. Create a shallow well in the center of the hummus with the back of a spoon. Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil, then sprinkle with paprika and chopped fresh parsley for a beautiful presentation and added flavor. Serve immediately with warm pita bread, fresh vegetables, or as part of a mezze platter.

tips: For the smoothest hummus, make sure to use good quality tahini and process the tahini-lemon mixture thoroughly before adding chickpeas.
Using ice water instead of room temperature water helps to create a lighter, fluffier texture by slightly emulsifying the mixture and preventing the tahini from seizing.
Hummus tastes even better after resting for an hour or two in the refrigerator, allowing the flavors to meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container for up to 5 days.
article: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil is a beloved Middle Eastern Appetizers dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
